text: In modern software development, teams use Continuous Integration (CI) to improve code quality and speed up delivery. The core idea is simple: whenever a developer pushes code changes to a shared repository, a CI pipeline automatically triggers. This pipeline first runs an automated build, compiling the new code and checking for errors. Then, it runs a series of tests to verify that everything works correctly. The main benefit is catching issues early, before they cause bigger problems later. If a build fails or a test breaks, the team is notified immediately, so they can fix the problem quickly. This process helps keep the codebase stable and reliable.
